Title: SlERF.G3-Like mediates a hierarchical transcriptional cascade to regulate ripening and metabolic changes in tomato fruit

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Stilbene synthase; type III polyketide synthase
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expressed under the fruit-specific E8 promoter in tomato; used as a metabolic engineering module together with SlERF.G3-Like. The paper states it is the key resveratrol biosynthetic gene. Source species and applied species are different.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit-specific transgene expression under E8 promoter
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvStSy driven by the E8 promoter enabled resveratrol compound production in tomato fruit. 2. Crossing E8:VvStSy with p35S:SlERF.G3-Like further increased total resveratrol derivatives from ~1.44 mg/g DW to ~2.31–2.43 mg/g DW. 3. The result supports flux redirection by SlERF.G3-Like to enhance VvStSy-based phenylpropanoid engineering.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables resveratrol derivative accumulation in tomato fruit, with higher accumulation when combined with SlERF.G3-Like
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful fruit-specific engineering gene for production of health-related resveratrol compounds in tomato; stacking with SlERF.G3-Like improves output while aiming to avoid vegetative penalties seen with constitutive resveratrol production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic expression under E8 promoter; genetic crossing with p35S:SlERF.G3-Like; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Previous p35S:VvStSy tomatoes reportedly showed poor fertility and stress tolerance; fruit-specific E8 expression was used to mitigate these penalties.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; 35S overexpression; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; gas chromatography ethylene measurement; dual-luciferase assays; yeast one-hybrid; metabolic profiling; genetic crossing for engineering
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression caused orange fruit phenotype and accelerated ripening/softening; no significant vegetative growth difference reported.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Potential protein interaction with SlFUL1 detected by yeast two-hybrid in supplementary data
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Specifically expressed at fruit ripening stages; induced by exogenous ethylene and inhibited by 1-MCP
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripening fruit; strongly reduced in rin mutant; induced by ethylene treatment
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as an intermediate hierarchical regulator linking master ripening control to ethylene synthesis, cell wall softening, and phenylpropanoid metabolism. Does not directly activate SlPSY1 or SlPDS based on dual-luc assays, so carotenoid changes are considered indirect. Used as a metabolic engineering tool to enhance VvStSy-derived resveratrol production.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: phenylpropanoid/flavonoid biosynthesis; ethylene biosynthesis; cell wall degradation/fruit softening; flux redirection into phenylpropanoid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Ethylene-responsive positive loop is supported: ethylene induces SlERF.G3-Like, and SlERF.G3-Like activates ethylene biosynthetic genes SlACS2 and SlACO1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates SlACS2 and SlACO1; activates SlPG2a and SlPL; previously shown to directly activate SlCHS1, SlFLS and SlF3H
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SlACS2; SlACO1; SlPG2a; SlPL; SlCHS1; SlFLS; SlF3H
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes fruit ripening, increases ethylene production, enhances softening, activates flavonoid pathway, and boosts resveratrol derivative accumulation when stacked with VvStSy
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-specific intermediate regulator and positive activator that redirects metabolic flux toward phenylpropanoid products during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR knockout delayed breaker stage and reduced ethylene production, while 35S overexpression accelerated ripening and increased ethylene. 2. SlERF.G3-Like directly activates SlACS2 and SlACO1, promoting climacteric ethylene biosynthesis. 3. SlERF.G3-Like directly activates SlPG2a and SlPL, promoting cell wall softening. 4. It positively regulates the phenylpropanoid/flavonoid pathway, including previously validated direct targets SlCHS1, SlFLS and SlF3H, and shifts flux away from lignin-related branches. 5. Crossing p35S:SlERF.G3-Like with E8:VvStSy increases total resveratrol derivatives to ~2.31–2.43 mg/g DW, supporting utility in metabolic engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ethylene induces SlERF.G3-Like expression; 1-MCP suppresses it

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; expression analysis in rin mutant; use of published ChIP-seq evidence
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Global ripening regulator; discussion notes targeting global regulators like SlRIN can cause many phenotypic changes including adverse effects such as flavor.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: ripening-associated master regulator; co-expressed with SlERF.G3-Like
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Directly binds CArG-box motifs in the SlERF.G3-Like promoter, especially CArG-box2 and CArG-box5. Functions upstream of SlERF.G3-Like in a hierarchical cascade.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: hierarchical control of ripening-associated metabolic changes via activation of SlERF.G3-Like
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates SlERF.G3-Like transcription by binding promoter CArG motifs
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Published ChIP-seq evidence referenced; RNA-seq context in this study
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: acts upstream to induce SlERF.G3-Like, thereby supporting ripening and associated metabolic reprogramming including phenylpropanoid changes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master upstream regulator in the hierarchical cascade controlling SlERF.G3-Like-dependent ripening and metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlRIN expression is tightly associated with SlERF.G3-Like during ripening. 2. SlRIN directly activates the SlERF.G3-Like promoter in transient assays. 3. Y1H and EMSA confirmed direct binding of SlRIN to CArG-box-containing promoter fragments, especially CArG-box2 and CArG-box5. 4. SlERF.G3-Like expression is strongly reduced in the rin mutant, supporting SlRIN as an upstream activator.
